title: ISic003367. I.Sicily inscription 003367
body:
Text encoding: Jonathan Prag
1 | τῶι Διὸς ἀγαθῶι Δ̣[---]-

3τᾶμ παίδων Μ̣[---]

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Preliminary text based upon autopsy and photograph, with reference to editions of Manganaro and Gallavotti ;
  • line.15: Manganaro 1981: ἦμ.ς ἐν ψ[- (it appears Manganaro could read the first part of the line, but nothing is now visible).
  • line.26: Manganaro 1981: χρησμ[ὸς], but there is no trace after the sigma
  • line.27: Manganaro 1981: φανείσ.[, but there is no trace after the sigma
  • line.28: Manganaro 1981: Παμφυλ[ία], but there is no trace after the upsilon
  • line.34: Manganaro 1981: γα(ν) δαιων [-, but the stone is clear, as Gallavotti 1983

translation

commentary

The stone was first published by Manganaro 1981 in association with ISic002754, the two seemingly having been discovered together, or at least in close proximity; the letter forms are essentially identical, and the texts were probably cut by the same hand. Manganaro 1981 and Parke 1986 noted the further potential relevance of ISic001040.

bibliography

  • 44.0738 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000796
  • 42.0824(b) https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000796
  • 36.0822 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000796
  • 35.0998.2 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000796
  • 31.0822 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000796
  • Manganaro 1981 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001411
  • Gallavotti 1983 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001473
  • Brugnone 1984-1985 239-240 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10002121
  • Peek 1985 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001471
  • Parke 1986 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001472
  • Manganaro 1992 473-484 ph https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001830
  • Manganaro 1994 111 no.22 fig.31 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001825
  • Porciani 2014 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001358

Letters are regular and deeply cut, with slightly prounced serifs at the end of strokes. Alpha is broad with straight bar, and occasional tendency to apex at the top; beta has equal sized, closed, shallow loops; epsilon has shorter middle bar and slightly outward pointing top and bottom bars; zeta is formed by a vertical with horizontals top and bottom; theta is slightly ovoid in form, with a complete cross bar; kappa has short, equal length tails; Mu has slightly inward leaning first and final strokes, with the middle strokes meeting well above the middle of the line; nu has a shorter right hand stroke, with the lower angle above the line; omicron is small and mid-line; Pi has a slightly shorter right hand vertical; rho has small closed eye, of varying form (rounded or elongated); sigma has slightly outward facing top and bottom storkes and the middle strokes are sometimes rounded at the join; upsilon has shallow spreading upper strokes; phi has a rounded eye, with extended vertical stroke; chi is consistnly slightly larger than other characters; omega open, slightly smaller and above the line, with horizontal tails. A horizontal stroke is visible above and the left of the first letter of lines 6 and 26.

Types list: Α type1 Α type1.1 Α type1.3 Β type2 Β type2.1 Β type2.2 Ε type2 Ε type2.3 Ε type2.3.1 Ε type2.3.2 Ε type2.4 Ε type2.5 Ε type2.6 Ζ type2 Θ type2 Θ type2.1 Κ type1 Κ type1.1 Κ type1.4 Μ type3 Μ type3.1 Ν type1 Ν type1.2 Ξ type1 Ξ type2 Ξ type2.1 Ο type1 Ο type1.2 Π type1 Π type1.1 Π type1.2 Π type2 Π type2.1 Π type2.2 Ρ type1 Ρ type1.1 Ρ type1.3 Σ tyoe3.2 Σ type3 Τ type2 Τ type2.1 Τ type2.2 Υ type2 Υ type2.1 Υ type2.2 Φ type2 Φ type2.2 Ω type1 Ω type1.1

Line 1-39 5-6 Interlineation line 1 to 2
